text: A 77-year-old man with metastatic prostate cancer reports worsening back pain despite taking 60 mg of morphine. He is irritable and demands breakthrough doses exactly on time. Most likely explanation?
rationale: Dx: Undertreated pain. Irritability and 'drug-seeking' behavior in cancer patients often indicate inadequate pain control rather than addiction[span_2](end_span). [span_3](start_span)Tx: Reassess and increase dose[span_3](end_span).

text: A 21-year-old man with a history of seizures presents with status epilepticus (seizure >5 mins). What is the most appropriate first-line pharmacotherapy?
rationale: Dx: Status Epilepticus[span_4](end_span). [span_5](start_span)Tx: Benzodiazepines (e.g., lorazepam) are first-line to terminate activity[span_5](end_span).

text: A 4-year-old boy has fever, odynophagia, neck stiffness, and trismus (inability to open mouth) after a URI. Likely diagnosis?
rationale: Dx: Retropharyngeal abscess (RPA)[span_6](end_span). [span_7](start_span)Common in kids 2-4; presents with trismus and neck pain on extension[span_7](end_span).
